paper_title: An investigation of timber frame housing in Vietnamese Mekong River Delta

entities.description: Common structures in many countries and a traditional construction method in the Vietnamese Mekong River Delta.
Traditional woodworking joints used to connect columns and beams in timber frame construction.
The field of study focusing on housing structures and building techniques.
Methodology involving direct observation and data collection at the location of local housings.
The process of measuring physical dimensions of buildings using specific tools.
A tool used for measuring the distance between two points in a building.
Technology used to capture details of building exteriors and structures via photography.
The collection of measurements, drawings, and photos from ten surveyed houses in the VMRD.
The measured distance between parallel floor beams in stilt housing.
The measured width of floor joists in stilt housing.
The distance between frame posts placed on the ground.
The total number of local houses investigated in the study.
Researcher from Mien Tay Construction University.
